description: Evaluates whether API endpoint implementations match their specs and meet quality standards

criteria.name: acceptance_criteria_coverage
criteria.description: Every acceptance criterion in the spec must have a corresponding implementation. Check each Given/When/Then statement and verify the code handles that exact scenario.
criteria.scoring: Count the acceptance criteria in the spec. For each one, determine if the code implements it (1) or not (0). Score = implemented / total. Example: 4 out of 5 ACs implemented = 0.8. If no spec exists, score is 0.0.

criteria.name: api_contract_fidelity
criteria.description: The implemented endpoint must match the spec's API contract: correct HTTP method, path, request body schema, response body schema, and status codes.
criteria.scoring: Check 5 elements: method (0.2), path (0.2), request schema (0.2), response schema (0.2), status codes (0.2). Partial matches score 0.1 per element. If no spec exists, score is 0.0.

criteria.name: error_handling
criteria.description: All error cases defined in the spec must be handled with correct HTTP status codes and RFC 7807 Problem Details format. Unhandled errors must not leak stack traces.
criteria.scoring: For each error case: (a) handled? +0.33 (b) correct status code? +0.33 (c) Problem Details format? +0.33. Score = total_correct / (error_cases * 3). Also check for generic try/catch that prevents stack trace leaks.

criteria.name: no_hallucinated_dependencies
criteria.description: All import statements must resolve to real files in the project or installed npm packages. All called functions must exist in the imported modules. No stub files with empty implementations.
criteria.scoring: Binary: 1.0 if all imports resolve and all called functions have real implementations. 0.0 if any import targets a non-existent or stub module, or any function call references a non-existent method.

criteria.name: type_safety
criteria.description: Code must use proper TypeScript types. No 'any' types, no unsafe type assertions (as any, as unknown), all function parameters and return types annotated, interfaces defined for all data shapes.
criteria.scoring: Start at 1.0. Deduct 0.1 for each: 'any' usage, missing type annotation, unsafe assertion, missing interface. Floor at 0.0.
